auth.scheme: Authorization: Bearer <token> on every API-host request
auth.external_links_rule: presigned external_link URLs embed their own short-lived credentials; the Authorization header MUST be omitted when downloading them

body.statement: SQL text; named parameter markers use :name syntax
body.parameters: [{name, value (string-encoded), type (SQL type, e.g. INT, STRING, DATE)}]
body.on_wait_timeout: CONTINUE (default) or CANCEL — what the server does if the wait elapses
body.disposition: INLINE (default, results <= 25 MiB) or EXTERNAL_LINKS (presigned chunk links, <= 100 GiB)

execute.success: 200 with {statement_id, status:{state}, manifest?, result?} — manifest/result present only once SUCCEEDED

poll.success: 200 with the same {statement_id, status, manifest?, result?} envelope

chunk.note: internal chunk links (next_chunk_internal_link) are relative API-host paths of this form and require Bearer auth

statement_states.failed_shape: status.error is {error_code, message}; status.sql_state carries the SQLSTATE code

manifest.fields: format, schema.column_count, schema.columns[], total_chunk_count, total_row_count, truncated, chunks[]

result.inline: result.data_array is a JSON array of rows; every value is string-encoded, SQL NULL is JSON null; next_chunk_internal_link when more chunks follow
result.external_links: result.external_links[] is [{chunk_index, row_offset, row_count, external_link (absolute presigned URL on another host), expiration (RFC3339), next_chunk_internal_link?, next_chunk_index?}]
result.traversal: download each external_link WITHOUT Authorization; follow next_chunk_internal_link (WITH Bearer auth) until absent; chunks must be contiguous — each chunk's row_offset equals the number of rows already assembled
result.external_body: for JSON_ARRAY format each presigned download body is the chunk's rows as a JSON array of string/null arrays
